statement: In the fixed hbar=1 Weyl convention, nonnegativity of the original coordinate Weyl kernel quadratic form is exactly nonnegativity of Op^W(sigma_omega).

kernelPositivityInputStatus.label: original Weyl kernel positivity input
kernelPositivityInputStatus.reason: The original Weyl kernel quadratic form is nonnegative for every |omega|<1/2.

kernelToOperatorIdentityStatus.label: kernel-to-operator identity
kernelToOperatorIdentityStatus.reason: The coordinate kernel is the integral kernel of Op^W(sigma_omega) in the fixed normalization.

proof: Use the fixed hbar=1 Weyl convention identifying the coordinate kernel with Op^W(sigma_omega).
A nonnegative integral-kernel quadratic form is exactly positivity of the corresponding Weyl operator.
